trajectory_summary: The call opened with a genuinely curious customer who had specific, answerable questions and a real interest in solar. Humphrey answered her questions reactively, introduced financing options he couldn't explain, and ended the call by assigning her homework (Credit Karma) instead of booking an appointment. The lead was live at 00:16 and gone by 04:16 with nothing scheduled.
outcome_dispute: The v1 tag is 'unknown' — this call should be tagged as 'lead_lost_no_appointment.' Ramona was qualified (homeowner, named utility, expressed interest, no hard DQ surfaced), the rep had a clear path to a booking, and the call ended with no appointment ask of any kind. This is not an ambiguous outcome.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Appointment ask — the call must end with a booking or an explicit DQ, not homework
primary_coaching_focus.quote: Yeah. You can just check it online, creditkarma.com. You can view your credit score there.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Humphrey described the specialist appointment at 03:23 and never asked for it. When Ramona said she didn't know her credit score, he assigned her a task and the call ended. This rep needs a hard rule internalized: every call closes with an appointment ask or a stated DQ reason. 'I don't know my credit score' is not a DQ — it is the moment to say 'let's get you on with our specialist and they'll run through that with you.' The Credit Karma redirect is the symptom; the missing appointment-ask habit is the disease.

key_moments.quote: Good afternoon, ma'am. This is Humphrey with Allian. I got a request that you're looking at solar panels for your home.
key_moments.stewart_read: Humphrey introduces himself as being 'with Allian,' not ION Solar. The codex is explicit: legitimacy must be established in the first 30 seconds. A customer who doesn't know who is calling has no reason to trust the answers that follow. This is not a minor slip — it is the foundation of the entire call, and it was laid wrong.

key_moments.quote: However, there are no tax incentive available. It ended last year.
key_moments.stewart_read: The codex's tax credit disclosure is specific: 'We can no longer guarantee the Tax Credit on purchased systems. It's possible they can still claim it if we install it prior to the end of the year. We do have purchase options available without the Tax Credit if needed.' Humphrey's version — 'It ended last year' — is a flat, unqualified negative that closes a door the codex leaves conditionally open. This is a schema drift on a customer-facing claim.

key_moments.quote: So so what is the out of pocket cost upfront?
key_moments.stewart_read: Ramona's repeated question is primarily a comprehension failure caused by the audio dropout she flagged at 01:58 ('I'm only hearing about one out of five words'). Humphrey never confirmed his answer landed after reconnecting. When she asked again, the correct move was to acknowledge the audio issue first, re-answer, and then pivot: 'Right now you're paying Santee Cooper every month regardless — what does that run you?' That pivot would have moved from her cost concern to bill anchoring, which is the emotional foundation the entire pitch needs. Instead Humphrey re-answered flatly and moved on. The bill_anchoring codex reference is valid here as a missed deployment, not a failure of the technique itself.

key_moments.quote: Well, because, that's for documentation and for qualification because, you do have two or two option on how to own the system that we offer. First one is you can buy it, and then second one is, like, a leasing program or some kind of a co ownership.
key_moments.stewart_read: Ramona asked a reasonable question — why does a no-cost program require a credit check? Humphrey's answer introduces buy vs. lease vs. co-ownership, which is closer-tier financing content. The codex is explicit: 'Technical questions, pricing, product specs — all are closer-tier content.' By opening this door, Humphrey created the follow-up question he couldn't answer ('So how does the leasing program work?') and the knowledge gap admission at 03:23. The scope creep at 02:56 is the root of the sequence that ends the call.
